title: RNA Extraction from 10µL Mouse Blood Samples Using KingFisher Flex
author: Petra Schneider
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.88fhztn
materials: Prepare reagents in kit only if opening a new MagMAX-96 Total RNA Isolation Kit (Cat#AM1830), Add the appropriate amount of ethanol or isopropanol to the wash buffers (see table below) and mark the bottles clearly by crossing out the word “concentrate”, ticking the small checkbox and writing your name/initials and the date. %s %s Note: some of the bench-top reagents crystallise when the ambient temperature is low (below ~18°C). To combat this, set a heat block to 25°C before beginning and place the room temperature reagents on it until ready to use, remembering to mix well!
Freeze samples at -70°C or immediately extract RNA.

steps.title: Dispense 20 µL of RNAlater into 0
steps.instruction: Dispense 20 µL of RNAlater into 0.2 mL strip tubes or 96-well plates, one well per sample. Cover tubes with lids, or plate with a plastic film ( the thick version, not the optical film for qPCRs – that one will NOT come off ). Note: using more RNAlater ends up inhibiting the downstream process.

steps.title: Take a 10 µL blood sample...
steps.instruction: Take a 10 µL blood sample (we use glass capillaries) and dispense the blood immediately into the RNAlater. Repeat for all samples.

steps.title: Mix with pipette and replace lid;...
steps.instruction: Mix with pipette and replace lid; or replace lid and mix by carefully tapping the tube.

steps.title: Get required plastics
steps.instruction: Get required plastics. You will need 6 Kingfisher DeepWell (DW; Cat# 95040460) plates, 2 Kingfisher 96 (KF; Cat# 97002540) plates and 1 DW tip comb (Cat# 97002534).

steps.title: Prepare bead mix
steps.instruction: Prepare bead mix. Mix 10µL of RNA binding beads ( vortex!) and 10µL of lysis/binding enhancer (total = 20 µL) per sample, in a sterile DNase/RNase-free eppendorf tube. Vortex the mixture after preparation, and again immediately before use. Note: this stuff sinks to the bottom quickly, vortex regularly if dispensing for many samples.

steps.title: Fill the plates as shown in the table
steps.instruction: Fill the plates as shown in the table: %s %s

steps.title: Connect the Kingfisher instrument to the...
steps.instruction: Connect the Kingfisher instrument to the computer using USB cable ( note: this should already be there) .

steps.title: Open the BindIt software
steps.instruction: Open the BindIt software . Select ‘Your Kingfisher Machine’ from the drop-down ‘connect’ list to connect to your Kingfisher instrument ( note: connection has likely established automatically) .

steps.title: Select the protocol (see step 22),...
steps.instruction: Select the protocol (see step 22), and click on the ‘start’ (green arrow) button on the BindIt software.

steps.title: Load the plates into the machine
steps.instruction: Load the plates into the machine. The machine will turn the turntable and prompt you to load the plates in the correct order. Press the ‘start’ button on the instrument to confirm you have loaded the current plate, this tells the machine to progress to the next plate. Make sure to line up the A1 well of the plate with the marked corner on the turntable position.

steps.title: Follow dispense step instructions
steps.instruction: Follow dispense step instructions. The run will begin once all plates are loaded. The instrument will prompt you to add the correct reagents at the dispense steps (grey cells in machine protocol, below). The first dispense step is roughly 2min after start-time. A guide to the approximate length of time until a step ends (so that you can work out how long between dispense steps) is also included in the machine protocol table below. Note: when adding beads - vortex first!

steps.title: Remove plates when the run has...
steps.instruction: Remove plates when the run has ended (as prompted by machine). Export AND SAVE the run details as a PDF (automatically opened in the software) – it contains details of the times each steps were begun as well as temperatures at frequent intervals, if something has gone wrong this may give you the answer why. Do not yet discard plates. Stop and switch off machine.

steps.title: Check, seal and label RNA extracts
steps.instruction: Check, seal and label RNA extracts. Check there is liquid in the elution plate ( if not, something has gone wrong!) . Seal the elution plate with a plastic film (make sure it is freezer-suitable and do not use the optical qPCR films – these do NOT come off) and label it carefully.

steps.title: Additional DNAse treatment (see step 22-30);
steps.instruction: Additional DNAse treatment (see step 22-30);

steps.title: diluting at least 1 volume extracted...
steps.instruction: diluting at least 1 volume extracted RNA + 4 volumes water before cDNA production to remove the effects of inhibitors

steps.title: if diluted before cDNA production, cDNA...
steps.instruction: if diluted before cDNA production, cDNA can go directly into qPCR without further dilution step.

steps.title: Discard all plates, except the elution plate
steps.instruction: Discard all plates, except the elution plate . If you haven’t used every well, you can keep them to be reused (be sure to use different, clean wells to the ones you have already used). And, if you do keep them for a while – remove liquids from used wells before storage.

steps.title: Optimized machine Protocol File to import...
steps.instruction: Optimized machine Protocol File to import into your BindIt software: RNA10uLMouseBlood.bdz Summary: Plates (add to BindIt software under the ‘Layout’ tab): Tip plate (KF 96 plate with DW 96 tip comb inserted) Sample plate (DW microtiter plate) Wash 1 (DW microtiter plate) Wash 2_1 (DW microtiter plate) Wash 2_2 (DW microtiter plate) Wash 2_3 (DW microtiter plate) Turbo DNase (DW microtiter plate) Elution (KF 96 plate) Protocol (add to BindIt software under the ‘Protocol’ tab): %s %s

steps.title: Prepare the DNAse mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the DNAse mix . Mix 49 µL Turbo DNase buffer ( mix well before use ) and 1 µL Turbo DNase enzyme per sample, in a sterile DNase/RNase-free eppendorf tube. Mix thoroughly with a pipette, and leave at room temperature until needed.

steps.title: Mix samples with lysis/binding solution
steps.instruction: Mix samples with lysis/binding solution. Label a Deep Well (DW) plate with the word ‘Sample’. Add 175 μL Lysis/Binding solution to the samples; pipette up and down ( multichannel comes in handy ), then move to the Deep Well 'Sample' plate. Add a further 175 μL of Lysis/Binding solution to each - now almost empty - sample tube to collect any remaining blood, mix, and transfer to the corresponding wells of the Deep Well 'Sample' plate. Note: we add a total of 350μL Lysis/Binding solution to each sample. There’s only 20mL per 100 samples available in the kit. Order AM8500 (100mL Lysis/Binding solution to make up the difference)

steps.title: We currently use RQ1 RNase-Free DNase Defrost
steps.instruction: We currently use RQ1 RNase-Free DNase Defrost. Take RQ1 RNase-Free DNase kit and samples (if frozen) from freezer. Thaw on ice. Vortex RQ1 buffer once defrosted.

steps.title: Program PCR machine
steps.instruction: Program PCR machine. You will need 37ºC for 30min and 65ºC for 10min.

steps.title: Prepare RQ1 reaction mix
steps.instruction: Prepare RQ1 reaction mix . Per sample you will need 2 µL RQ1 10x reaction buffer and 4 µL RQ1 enzyme. Prepare RQ1 reaction mix for all samples and aliquot 6 µL per sample into PCR tubes/plate.

steps.title: Add RNA extract
steps.instruction: Add RNA extract. Add 14 µL of RNA extract to each tube/well with RQ1 reaction mix (total volume 20 µL). Mix gently, Seal tubes/plate.

steps.title: 37C 30 min
steps.instruction: 37C 30 min. Place tubes/plate in PCR machine. Run at 37ºC for 30 min.

steps.title: Spin down
steps.instruction: Spin down. Briefly centrifuge to spin down contents.

steps.title: Add stop solution
steps.instruction: Add stop solution. Add 2 µL RQ1 stop solution to each sample.

steps.title: 65C 10 min
steps.instruction: 65C 10 min. Place tubes/plate in PCR machine. Run at 65ºC for 10 min.

steps.title: Cool to 4 °C for 5...
steps.instruction: Cool to 4 °C for 5 mins before using cDNA production, or store samples at -20ºC (short term) or -70ºC (longer term)
